failures.description: The caller violated a language binding API precondition, such as passing a null required pointer. It is not a wire failure.
A decimal integer string is empty, signed, padded, fractional, or contains non-digits.
A uint64 representation exceeds 20 UTF-8 bytes or its canonical decimal value exceeds 18446744073709551615; the byte-length check precedes lexical validation.
A binding received a cyclic in-memory value that cannot be represented as JSON.
A string contains invalid Unicode, including an unpaired surrogate.
A number is NaN or infinite and therefore is not a JSON number.
A finite binary64 value is integer-valued outside the safe-integer range and its RFC 8785 serialization does not retain a decimal point; use a contract-declared string representation.
A binding received a language-specific value that has no JSON representation.
Raw JSON is malformed; a binding should report an offset or path when available.
A closed core object contains an undeclared field.
Input exceeds a declared byte, depth, string, member, array, or token bound.
Raw JSON contains the same object member name more than once.
A previously observed durable position identity is reused with a different stable batch_id or business digest binding.
A digest does not use the required sha256 lowercase hexadecimal representation.
A declared business digest differs from the digest computed over the frozen canonical projection.
A message is bound to a gateway, session, epoch, or credential generation that is no longer current.
A message declares expires_at_ms earlier than sent_at_ms.
The explicit evaluation_time_ms is equal to or later than expires_at_ms.
Data-loss evidence violates first_lost_position <= last_lost_position < earliest_retained_position.
One message repeats the same stream_id and stream_epoch cursor identity.
The declared protocol or contract version is not supported by the selected profile.
The experimental transcript is selected where production key lifecycle, verifier ownership, or trusted-adapter configuration is unavailable.
The selected origin model requires authentication material that is absent.
Authentication material is malformed or does not satisfy the selected experimental origin profile.
A runtime version is not a strict Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 string.
A capability declaration does not grant execution; the governed job or edge policy denied it.
A Thing Model attempted to declare direct execution instead of a governed job.
A key is reused within or across the property, point, or capability namespaces of one Thing Model, or a parameter key is repeated within one capability.
The requested property key is absent from the supplied Thing Model.
The requested point key is absent from the supplied Thing Model.
The requested capability key is absent from the supplied Thing Model.
The consumer verifier command line is malformed or selects mutually exclusive modes.
The composite consumer Action was not invoked at the exact peeled release commit recorded by the consumer lock.
The imported and pending source set is not the exact required-artifact closure declared by the lock, or complete-consumer is claimed before that closure is imported.
The authenticated release archive does not have the single locked root or bounded entry layout.
The authenticated archive cannot be inspected or contains an escaping path, link, device, or another unsupported entry type.
The downloaded release bundle SHA-256 differs from the consumer-reviewed lock.
The downloaded release bundle size differs from the consumer-reviewed lock.
The one exact release URL in the lock could not be fetched; no alternate source is selected automatically.
An imported consumer artifact no longer has the release digest recorded by the lock.
An imported consumer path is not a regular file contained by the consumer root.
An imported consumer artifact required by the lock is absent.
The selected AetherContracts consumer lock is absent.
A consumer lock repeats an import path or declares one source as both imported and pending.
The consumer lock is not strict JSON or violates the closed v1 alpha 1 lock contract.
An import or pending import is absent from the release manifest or declares a different digest.
The release manifest bytes differ from the SHA-256 fixed by the consumer lock.
The manifest contract name or release version differs from the consumer lock.
The release manifest is not strict JSON or has an invalid closed artifact declaration.
The digest-pinned release manifest is absent from the consumer or authenticated release root.
An imported or pending source in the authenticated release archive differs from its locked digest.
A locked source path in the authenticated release is not a contained regular file.
An imported or pending source declared by the lock is absent from the authenticated release.
The release manifest disagrees with the lock about production status, legacy default, or physical-control exclusion.
